posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday community!

Another question related to mirror columns but this time on a timeline column.

What I try to do is to find the minimum end dates of items linked to another item as illustrated below:

image896×121 5.13 KB

The only thing I succeed to display is the maximum end date using “show the latest”:

If I use “show the earliest” it will consider the minimum start date and not end date as expected.

Is there a way to do that? I would like to avoid the split in 2 columns dates (start / end).

Thanks in advance!

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @ECH - if you’re open to using apps, Smart Timeline + Duration might be helpful for your use case. It offers 20+ automation options that can make managing your dates and timelines a lot easier. Feel free to check it out, and I’m here if you have any questions or need a hand.

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Avo. Thanks for your proposal and for the time to answer me but marketplace is blocked by my company.
